meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Analytics Setup to Solve SaaS Blindspots
content.seo_description: Master SaaS analytics with this expert checklist. Learn how to bridge the knowledge gap using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-value client data.
content.answer_block: To solve SaaS blindspots, implement a event-driven analytics architecture. By integrating lightweight SDKs with automated event tracking via Claude Code, you transform raw traffic into actionable user behavioral data. This setup provides the professional, data-backed reporting clients demand, ensuring you move from a mere coder to a strategic partner.
content.intro:
You’ve shipped the MVP, but the dashboard is a ghost town. Your client is asking for conversion rates and user retention metrics, and you’re staring at a blank screen, realizing you didn't build for visibility. The 'no SaaS knowledge' trap is the silent killer of freelance careers, turning high-quality builds into invisible products that fail to prove their own worth.

But here is the secret: analytics isn't about complex engineering; it's about strategic hooks. By treating analytics as a first-class citizen in your development lifecycle, you don't just track clicks—you track value. This checklist is your blueprint to installing professional-grade observability into every project, ensuring you never have to guess what your users are doing again.

steps.title: Define Core Conversion Events
steps.description: Identify the 3-5 critical actions that signal value (e.g., signup, checkout, feature usage) before writing any tracking code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an analytics event schema for a SaaS platform. Define 'user_signup', 'subscription_started', and 'feature_clicked' events with properties for user_id, timestamp, and source_medium.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a TypeScript interface for the defined analytics schema and place it in src/types/analytics.ts'

steps.title: Configure Universal Analytics Provider
steps.description: Initialize a provider-agnostic analytics wrapper to avoid vendor lock-in and simplify future migrations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a singleton analytics service class that wraps PostHog or Mixpanel, providing a standard 'track(event, properties)' method for the whole app.

steps.title: Implement Middleware for Route Tracking
steps.description: Automatically capture page views and user flow without polluting your primary business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Next.js middleware that captures page view events and sends them to the analytics service, excluding static assets and admin routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit middleware.ts --instruction 'Inject analytics page_view tracking logic into the request pipeline'

steps.title: Automate User Identification
steps.description: Ensure every event is tied to a unique user profile to allow for cohort analysis and retention tracking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a hook 'useIdentifyUser' that triggers the analytics identity method upon successful login or user session authentication.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create src/hooks/useIdentifyUser.ts and integrate with current Auth provider'

steps.title: Validate Event Payload Integrity
steps.description: Automate testing to ensure events fire correctly during development so you don't ship broken data to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the analytics service to verify that event payloads match the schema defined in Step 1.

pro_tips: Always use a 'staging' analytics project key to prevent test data from polluting your client's production dashboard.
Implement a 'debug mode' flag that logs all analytics payloads to the browser console for real-time verification during QA.
Avoid tracking raw PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in event properties; hash emails if unique identifiers are strictly necessary.
Capture 'error_occurred' events as a custom event type to monitor client-side crashes before users report them.
Use server-side tracking for critical events like payments to ensure they aren't blocked by ad-blockers or browser privacy settings.
resources: PostHog Event Schema Best Practices: https://posthog.com/docs/data/events
content.next_steps: Once your tracking is live, build a simple 'Client Insights' dashboard using your analytics provider's API to show your client the direct impact of your work.
content.conversion_block: Stop building blindly and start delivering data-backed ROI. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
